item.title: What Trump's generic drug tariffs could mean, experts say
item.description: Health policy experts say higher import duties could increase costs and strain supplies while offering no guarantee manufacturers will shift production to the U.S.

item.title: Senate committee advances Clayton's nomination for intelligence chief
item.description: The Senate Intelligence Committee advanced Jay Clayton's nomination for director of national intelligence, despite opposition from Democrats.

item.title: House and Senate take different paths to avoid shutdown before midterms
item.description: Another deadline to fund the government is looming this fall in Congress, and lawmakers seem eager to avoid another costly impasse ahead of the midterm elections.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha makes landfall on Gulf Coast. See its path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Latest on confirmation processes for Clayton, Blanche
item.description: President Trump's pick for director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton, has advanced to the next stage in the Senate's nomination process. Meanwhile, another Senate committee will vote Thursday on Mr. Trump's pick for attorney general, Todd Blanche. Jeffrey Epstein victims urge senators to reject Blanche. CBS News' Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb a bridge or power plant for every ship Iran attacks
item.description: The Pentagon keeps insisting the Strait of Hormuz is open, but with Iranian attacks continuing, one shipping insider says "nothing is going through."

item.title: Should you lock a mortgage rate before the July Fed meeting? Pros and cons to consider now
item.description: There are major pros and cons associated with a mortgage interest rate lock to know now. Here are four to consider.

item.title: "Nothing is going through" the Strait of Hormuz, shipping insider says
item.description: Despite U.S. assertions, the CEO of a maritime risk firm says "there is no guarantee of safe passage" through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 5,700 more deaths than usual recorded in France during heat wave
item.description: France experienced at least 5,700 more deaths than usual during a historic heat wave last month, according to the public health agency.

item.title: What the Arizona primary results mean for the 2026 midterm election
item.description: Arizona candidates backed by President Trump swept the state's primary elections on Tuesday. CBS News' Zak Hudak has more.

item.title: Trump attends dignified transfer of soldiers killed in Iranian attacks
item.description: President Trump attended a dignified transfer honoring the four U.S. Army soldiers killed in the Middle East in recent days during the ongoing war with Iran.

item.title: Democrats probe DOJ opinion that eases institutionalization of mentally ill
item.description: Democrats in the Senate and House are investigating the origins of a DOJ legal opinion that would make it easier for states to keep people with mental illness in institutions, rather than helping them to live at home.

item.title: Dignified transfer for 4 U.S. troops killed in the Middle East amid Iran war
item.description: President Trump, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and family members attended a dignified transfer on Wednesday honoring the four U.S. Army soldiers killed in the Middle East in recent days during the war with Iran. Mugo Odigwe anchored CBS News' coverage.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $12,000 short-term CD account can earn now
item.description: Have $12,000 to transfer into a short-term CD account now? Here's how much interest you can earn if you do.

item.title: Nolan Wells' cause of death undetermined, family's autopsy shows
item.description: Nolan Wells, 18, was found dead just off Horn Island in Mississippi after enjoying a day out with friends on July 4.

item.title: U.S. reaches deal with Saudis for nuclear energy commercial agreements
item.description: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia are expected to sign a deal for U.S. companies to run nuclear power for the Saudis, potentially allowing the Saudis to enrich their own fuel for nuclear reactors.

item.title: DOJ sues 2 Chinese companies over immersion water heaters that can catch on fire
item.description: The Justice Department is trying to force the two Chinese companies to recall immersion water heaters used for bathtubs and pools.

item.title: Student murdered by ex thought she was meeting him one last time
item.description: Catina Salarno's sister, Nina Salarno, became a prosecutor in California to warn others about a common tactic abusers use.

item.title: Horse trainer discovers pre-dug grave of missing California teen
item.description: After seeing a freshly dug hole while conditioning a horse, the equestrian found out later it was used to bury 17-year-old Justine Vanderschoot.

item.title: Sneak peek: The Boy Across the Street
item.description: ENCORE: A college student is killed by her boyfriend who lived right across the street. Her family's long battle to keep him in prison. "48 Hours" correspondent Erin Moriarty reports Saturday, July 25 at 10/9c on CBS and streaming on Paramount+.

item.title: Watch: Sen. Bennet rails against trade chief for Trump policy's impact on farmers
item.description: Sen. Michael Bennet, a Democrat from Washington, challenged some of U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer's assertions about the successes of President Trump's trade policies and their impact on farmers.

item.title: Ryanair passenger says he was nearly sucked out of broken window during flight
item.description: A 61-year-old Serbian businessman who was traveling on a Ryanair flight says he was almost sucked out of the plane when a window broke. Ryanair contests parts of the man's account. CBS News' Tom Hanson has more.

item.title: Sneak peek: Justine's Voice
item.description: ENCORE: After a teenager is strangled and buried alive, her family takes on the fight to keep her convicted killers behind bars. "We're her voice" says Justine's sister. "48 Hours" correspondent Natalie Morales reports Saturday, July 25 at 9/8c on CBS and streaming on Paramount+.

item.title: Lung cancer in nonsmokers: "A women's health crisis"
item.description: Lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer death in American women — and a growing share of new cases are in women who've never smoked.

item.title: Kalshi debuts a midterm election hub to predict race outcomes
item.description: The site claims its hub will provide accurate insights into election outcomes based on traders' bets.

item.title: Trump ally Andy Biggs wins GOP nomination for Arizona governor, AP projects
item.description: Trump-endorsed Rep. Andy Biggs will take on Democratic Arizona Gov. Katie Hobbs in November.

item.title: Andy Burnham takes the reins as U.K.'s 7th prime minister in a decade
item.description: Having been asked by King Charles III to form a new government, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has become Britain's 7th prime minister in a decade.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Half of Young Adults Still Live at Home | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description: Half of American adults under 30 are now living with their parents. Is this the new normal, or just a temporary consequence of today's housing market? Regardless, Jill advises young adults living at home to at least offer to chip in financially. Plus, Mark explains why obsessing over minimizing taxes can lead to costly investing mistakes, and Jill shares her thoughts on how friends with different income levels should approach splitting the cost of dinners, vacations, and group outings.

item.title: Can You Hack Your Brain to Stop Impulse Buying? | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description:
"Dopamine sites" are on the rise in South Korea, allowing people to simulate the thrill of online shopping or food delivery without spending any actual money. Could this be the fix for compulsive spenders? Plus, Jill helps caller Danielle navigate a financial crossroads: after taking a pay cut to make work and life more manageable, she's now facing higher-than-expected college costs for her child while trying to stay on track for retirement.
Have a money question? Email askjill@jillonmoney.com.
